rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical monofluoroalkylation/cyclization of N-arylacrylamides with dimethyl 2-fluoromalonate to construct monofluorinated 2-oxindoles via a ferrocene-mediated anodic radical pathway.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ which acts as a SET mediator to oxidize the fluoromalonate carbanion to a monofluoroalkyl radical; cathode reduces MeOH to MeO- and H2.

key_intermediates.name: Carbon-centered cyclohexadienyl/benzylic radical 33 after radical addition and cyclization onto arene

electron_flow: At the anode, Cp2Fe is oxidized to Cp2Fe+; at the cathode MeOH is reduced to MeO- + 1/2 H2. MeO- deprotonates dimethyl 2-fluoromalonate to carbanion 31, which Cp2Fe+ oxidizes via SET to monofluoroalkyl radical 32; radical addition to the acrylamide alkene followed by intramolecular cyclization onto the arene gives radical 33, which is oxidized and deprotonated to deliver the monofluorinated oxindole.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of substrate 1, product 3, mediator Cp2Fe, and fluoromalonate 2
probe_reagents.observation: E_p/2: Cp2Fe 0.46 V, carbanion 31 0.45 V, fluoromalonate 2 1.21 V, substrate 1 1.79 V, product 3 1.64 V (vs Ag/AgCl); Cp2Fe+ can oxidize carbanion 31 but not 2, 1, or 3, supporting mediated SET via the deprotonated malonate.

notes: Mechanism is proposed based on CV data and literature precedent (refs 13,14); no radical trapping (TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT studies are reported. The same mediated monofluoroalkyl radical is invoked for related transformations in the paper including spirocyclization to 2-azaspiro[4.5]decanes, indole[2,1-a]isoquinoline formation, and 1,2-aryl migration from allyl alcohols to 4-fluorobutanones.
